Friend,

On behalf of everyone here at the Republican National Committee, I want to personally send a heartfelt message of appreciation in celebration of this Mother’s Day.

Today we honor the incredible contributions of those important women in all our lives who ground us, teach us, and protect us.

As a proud mother of two, I can attest to motherhood being one of the greatest blessings in my life, but I also recognize the challenges faced by American families over this past year. The mothers in our homes, our workplaces, and our healthcare facilities have continued to lead with courage, and we are all incredibly grateful.

Reflecting on this day, I am proud to represent the RNC, where we are leading the charge fighting for America’s children and the preservation of American families. We are the party of country and family.

As we continue to blaze a trail for our children, we are fighting to preserve our incredible Conservative values for generations to come. Thank you to all the mothers for building the foundations for the future leaders of our great nation.

Thank you,

Ronna McDaniel

Chairman, Republican National Committee

name: Nancy Pelosi
subject: Happy Mother's Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nancy Pelosi for Congress
body:
Dear friends:On this Mother’s Day, I send gratitude and the warmest wishes to moms, grandmothers, and mother figures throughout America.
We honor the sacrifice and service of America’s mothers in uniform, including mothers who are away from their families on this Mother’s Day as they serve our country.
As we move from this tumultuous chapter in our history, America owes an enormous debt of gratitude to our frontline heroes – many of whom are working moms: health care providers, first responders, our teachers who have been champions for America’s children...and all essential workers keep our country going.
Because of the hard work of our VIPs – our Volunteers In Politics including many hard-working moms – who gave us the White House, House, and Senate, we won and delivered.
Because Democrats won, Help Is Here to crush the virus.
Today, my heart is full in knowing that the COVID rescue package that you made possible will cut child poverty in half.
We are just getting started – more help is on the way.
On Mother’s Day and every day, we miss the mothers who are no longer with us but continue to inspire us.
Happy Mother’s Day and God bless America!
NANCY
Nancy Pelosi is the Speaker of the U.S. House of Representatives.
As the top Democrat in the House, she's fighting to help working families and protect America from the dangerous agenda of Mitch McConnell and Kevin McCarthy.
We know you're getting a lot of emails -- but it's only because what we're facing right now as a country is so important.

subject: my thoughts on this Mother’s Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Congress
body:
| [](https://act.myngp.com/el/mez7dxEbDthnXxRxHeereZZakgH7gfmhkA2DjhgH_nU=/JWh2aHH3ARKaEyWUb0CVKFJ-8X9XoB3s5YxPNcSIaf4=?refcode=BLU-SPAN-20210509-1-EM&amount=25)
---
| Good morning and Happy Mother's Day, Peter!

Today, as I celebrate the day with my daughters, I'm thinking about the future and how my children and their generation will help shape it.

**Giving children the opportunity to succeed is why I am devoted to tirelessly advocating for our nation 's students from the day they enter school to the day they graduate.**

While serving in Congress, I introduced bipartisan legislation to allow the early childhood Head Start programs to receive federal work-study dollars. I also co-sponsored legislation to encourage more women and minority students to enter STEM fields.

**We all know what high-quality education can do to the lives of students.** You can hear it in the voices of children like my daughters as they describe what new, magical things they learned in the classroom.

As long as I am serving in Congress, I will continue to fight for educational opportunities for all, no matter where they live.

In addition to ensuring our children are getting the best possible education, we also need to make sure that the earth they inherit is healthy.

That's why I am also committed to **protecting natural resources for our children and generations to come** by investing in alternative and renewable energy sources and decreasing air and water pollution.

In my time as Congresswoman of VA-07, I've led many roundtables and panels with Central Virginia conservationists and farmers to discuss how we can work together to improve our region's conservation practices. For our children to enjoy our beautiful district for generations to come, we need to find climate solutions collaboratively.

**Our children are the future. And I believe if we give them the right tools to succeed, the future will be brighter than ever.**

I hope that you all enjoy this Mother's Day.

With appreciation,

Abigail Spanberger

| | Hi, it’s Nevada Joan Lee (aka Nevie), Susie’s daughter. First, let me wish you and your family a **Happy Mother’s Day!** I hope you’re enjoying today relaxing, spending family time together or, if you’re like us, enjoying the outdoors. My Mom doesn’t know that I’m writing to you but, I wanted to take this chance to show her how much my brother Cody and I appreciate and love her. Growing up, she was there whenever we needed her to be and always made sure we were happy. She knew that it wouldn’t be easy to raise two kids while working full-time, but _she did it anyway._ **My mother taught me the importance of being independent and strong.** I know it may sound cliché and all but I keep those values in the back of my head whenever things get tough or I need a little extra kick. When my Mom told me that she was running for Congress, I was a little hesitant at first; this was a big change for our family that would require sacrifices from everyone. But, we knew that these sacrifices were a small price to pay in order to see my Mom do what she does best: _**serving our community.**_ I have to admit, campaigning was pretty fun for all of us. Being able to talk to neighbors, meet new people, and see people trust in my Mom as much as I do was awesome. On the night of my Mom’s first election when we found out she won, one takeaway was particularly strong for me: **_women can run for office and they can win._** Channeling the same courage my mother showed, **I decided to run for president of my own college Democrats chapter and won!** Those values of independence and strength that she instilled in me at a young age really came through, too. _(I’ll also never forget calling her that night to share the news and celebrate!)_ There’s so many reasons why I’m thankful for my Mom today. This email isn’t even the tip of the iceberg but, I think it’s a pretty good place to start. _Peter, I know firsthand how difficult Mother’s Day can be. I lost my mom, and I often get sad when I think about her. If Mother’s Day is a difficult moment for you, please know that you aren’t alone._
* * *

This Mother’s Day, I’m feeling an immense amount of gratitude to so many people. I jotted down a list the other day to share with you, Peter.
1. I’m thankful to have been raised by such a resilient, brave mother. We faced domestic violence in our household, and my mom helped my siblings and me move onwards and upwards from difficult circumstances. She enrolled in community college later in life to become a registered nurse because she so deeply believed in caring for others. I miss her every day.

2. I’m so lucky to be the mother to two strong daughters. They inspired me to run for office -- because I want to do everything I can to build a better future for them. They keep me laughing and smiling every day.

3. I’m grateful for all caregivers, both traditional and non-traditional. Raising children really does take a village of support -- family, friends, neighbors, teachers, nurses, babysitters, and more. Especially in this pandemic, I think we have all realized how important having a support system is to us all.

4. I’m so appreciative of elected officials across the country who fight for parents and children. From local school board members seeking more resources for afterschool programs to members of Congress passing paid family leave, we are lucky to have leaders who believe in helping each other out.

This is a short but extensive list -- I could go on all day about all the people I’m thankful for. Today, make sure you send love and appreciation to all those who support mothers every single day.

Warmly,

Christy

| | Today is Mother’s Day, and like many of you, I spend the day thinking about the women in my life and everything they taught me.

I would not be where I am today without them, and many of the strong convictions I hold are because of them. One, in particular, is my strong belief that healthcare is a human right.

19 years ago, my mother passed away from breast cancer. My wife Maria has also bravely fought breast cancer. _They were both lucky because they had health insurance. A lot of Americans can’t say the same._

**My mother and wife’s experiences have helped define my stance on healthcare. Their story,_and the story of millions of other Americans, are why I support Medicare for All._**

[On this Mother’s Day, I want to hear from you. Take our survey and let me know if you support Medicare for All, and if you do, why:](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/28906400/284713528/-1949931764?refcode=TN_EM_SV_1MO_210509_1_1&refcode2=50134d39-e9b0-eb11-85aa-0050f237abef&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84Njk2MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MDEzNGQzOS1lOWIwLWViMTEtODVhYS0wMDUwZjIzN2FiZWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lhMCI2IucxojnjJN-ziEw3aVtSpXWSflVC5h1WB06VA=&emci=63884e68-51af-eb11-85aa-0050f237abef&emdi=50134d39-e9b0-eb11-85aa-0050f237abef&ceid=15112925)

I support Medicare for all because:
* No Americans should worry about losing health insurance when they lose a job.

* No Americans should hesitate to retire just because they’re years away from Medicare.

**It’s just wrong that over 33 million Americans lack insurance, especially during this pandemic.**

[You know where I stand, but I want to know if you feel the same way. Tom Nelson

clean_body: | | Today is Mother’s Day, and like many of you, I spend the day thinking about the women in my life and everything they taught me. I would not be where I am today without them, and many of the strong convictions I hold are because of them. One, in particular, is my strong belief that healthcare is a human right. 19 years ago, my mother passed away from breast cancer. My wife Maria has also bravely fought breast cancer. They were both lucky because they had health insurance. A lot of Americans can’t say the same. My mother and wife’s experiences have helped define my stance on healthcare. Their story,and the story of millions of other Americans, are why I support Medicare for All. | | I support Medicare for all because: No Americans should worry about losing health insurance when they lose a job. No Americans should be one fall or one slip away from bankruptcy. No Americans should hesitate to retire just because they’re years away from Medicare. It’s just wrong that over 33 million Americans lack insurance, especially during this pandemic. Thank you for hearing me out, and happy Mother’s Day. Tom Nelson | | | | = Tom Nelson serves as County Executive for Outagamie, one of the worst-hit COVID areas in the nation. Local officials have been leading our country through the pandemic because Donald Trump and Ron Johnson have failed. Tom is running for U.S. Senate because we need real leaders in Washington who will take COVID seriously. And if we can take on a pandemic, we can take on anything. subject: I’ve seen first-hand how Washington has failed folks like us
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Malcolm PAC
body:
#

Friend,
I know what it’s like to work a minimum wage job, face a mountain of student loan debt, and struggle tooth and nail for stable housing.
I grew up watching my mom ration her insulin, so trust that I’ve seen first-hand how Washington has failed folks like us.
And if we’re going to fix what’s broken, then we need more working people in the U.S. Senate.
**But here’s the thing — a lot of folks think that a gay Black man from the North Philly projects can’t win. And that swearing off all money from corporate PACs is crazy.**
[Will you help me prove them wrong by making a contribution to our campaign today? name: Sarah Godlewski
subject: I believe (why I'm running for Senate)
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY SARAH FOR WISCONSIN
body:
Hey Peter,

I'll be honest, when I first announced my campaign to take on Ron Johnson, I wasn't sure how people would react. But so far the reaction from Wisconsinites across the state has been humbling and encouraging.

I launched this campaign because I think Washington could use more leaders who will look out for the best interests of working families.

Winning this race won't be easy. _Inside Elections_ is calling Wisconsin a battleground race AND Ron Johnson has the backing of Donald Trump and national Republicans who will stop at nothing to defeat us.

[**If you're ready to say goodbye to Ron Johnson in 2022, will you chip in $5, or whatever you can, right away to help us kick-off our campaign?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/28911210/284729937/-1605854856?gpsid=50_130_18478&refcode=em21_210427_WS3_fnd_gq_New__SG_ettx&utm_campaign=em21_210427_WS3_fnd_gq_New__SG_ettx&utm_medium=email&utm_source=sgod&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODI4NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwZWM0ZTEzOC0wMWIxLWViMTEtODVhYS0wMDUwZjIzN2FiZWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dy2JArNbGH9YNDcZ_twqsgU0PetGJDO5Qsaajtmwz7k=)

Ron Johnson isn't fighting for Wisconsin families, he's only fighting for the wealthy and well-connected. Well, I say working families deserve better.

I believe every _single_ Wisconsinite deserves a fair shot to succeed. I believe our students should be able to graduate from college or trade school without a mountain of crippling debt. I believe we can raise the minimum wage to $15 an hour. And I believe that, together, **we can defeat Ron Johnson.**

It's all hands on deck if we're going to flip this seat -- especially if we want a shot at expanding the Democratic majority in the Senate. So, I need to know you're with me in the fight.

[**It's time to elect a Senator who will stand up for Wisconsin families, chip in $5, or whatever you can, right away to help spread our campaign message across the state.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/28911216/284729943/-1605854856?gpsid=50_130_18478&refcode=em21_210427_WS3_fnd_gq_New__SG_ettx&utm_campaign=em21_210427_WS3_fnd_gq_New__SG_ettx&utm_medium=email&utm_source=sgod&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODI4NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwZWM0ZTEzOC0wMWIxLWViMTEtODVhYS0wMDUwZjIzN2FiZWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dy2JArNbGH9YNDcZ_twqsgU0PetGJDO5Qsaajtmwz7k=)

Thank you,

Sarah

#

$337,896
Is that how much we’ve raised so far this month to hold this seat? Maybe it’s the amount of money that’s already been spent by Super PACs to defeat us?
Both are wrong.
$337,896 is the amount of money that our first opponent — a major National Republican and Trump donor — **is spending this month on TV ads** announcing his campaign to unseat Mark.
Now look, we are not shy to opponents spending big money to defeat us. To be honest, we expect our opponents to go big and spend more than us to take back this seat.
But what we didn’t expect was to have an opponent spending $337,896 on TV ads 18 months before the election.
So we’re going to do what we do best — fight together.
$10 may not sound like a lot compared to $337,896 but if everyone reading this pitches in just a little right now we can have Mark’s back and hold this seat.
[**So if you are able, will you chip in $10 right now to let Mark’s new opponent know that he can’t just walk into this race and buy this seat? Both are wrong. $337,896 is the amount of money that our first opponent — a major National Republican and Trump donor — is spending this month on TV ads announcing his campaign to unseat Mark. Now look, we are not shy to opponents spending big money to defeat us. To be honest, we expect our opponents to go big and spend more than us to take back this seat. But what we didn’t expect was to have an opponent spending $337,896 on TV ads 18 months before the election. So we’re going to do what we do best — fight together. $10 may not sound like a lot compared to $337,896 but if everyone reading this pitches in just a little right now we can have Mark’s back and hold this seat. - If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly: | - | - ---|--- - | - ---|--- - | - ---|--- $10 at a time. **Partnership to Fight Infectious Disease**

**Former New Jersey Governor Chris Christie addresses communities about the importance of getting vaccinated against COVID-19.**

As vaccines become increasingly available across the country, communities are working hard to ensure that everyone has accurate information about the vaccines so that anyone who wants to be vaccinated has the opportunity to do so. In the meantime, it is important that we continue to follow guidelines for social distancing, mask wearing, and washing hands.

**Christie: Biden spending like a 15-year-old with no credit card limit**

name: PramilaforCongress.com
subject: Did you see what Rachel Maddow said last week?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
#
Peter, Pramila was on The Rachel Maddow Show last week to discuss her work to end the filibuster and deliver for the people. Maddow even said Pramila is, “one of the most powerful and interesting players” in Washington right now.
**[Pramila is working hard to end the filibuster and win progressive policies in Congress, but she can only do it with your support. Chip in $3 right now to help Pramila continue leading on the bold, transformative change we need.](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/2019?refcode=em20210509-276&t=1&akid=276%2E420207%2E5dSXHt)**
[](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/2019?refcode=em20210509-276&t=2&akid=276%2E420207%2E5dSXHt)
In solidarity,
Team Jayapal
P.S. If you didn’t see Pramila’s email on the filibuster from Thursday, check it out below!
\----Forwarded Message----
From: Pramila Jayapal
Date: 5/6/21
Subject: The movement to end the filibuster is growing
Peter, in a few minutes, [**I am going to ask you to rush a donation toward our people-powered movement for transformative change.**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/2019?refcode=em20210509-276&t=3&akid=276%2E420207%2E5dSXHt) But first, I wanted to explain why our efforts to take down a significant roadblock to real progress are so important.
Last week, President Biden finished his first 100 days in office. As he addressed the nation, he outlined the incredible, life-saving work that his administration had accomplished so far — from shots in arms and money in pockets to a historic slate of executive actions on a range of issues.
And today, as Chair of the Congressional Progressive Caucus, I want to share what WE accomplished and passed in the House during that same time:
* The For the People Act to end voter suppression and get big money out of politics
* The Equality Act to protect LGBTQIA+ people all across the country from discrimination in housing, health care, education, and other public accommodations
* The Dream Act, which provides a path to citizenship for immigrants who came to this country as children
* The Paycheck Fairness Act to guarantee equal pay for equal work
* Gun safety legislation to save lives
* The George Floyd Justice in Policing Act to take on police brutality
* DC statehood
* Environmental legislation to protect our public lands
* The PRO Act to protect workers and their right to organize
* The NO BAN and Access to Counsel Acts to protect civil rights and civil liberties
* And that's not even the entire list, Peter!

**Each one of these bills is urgent, necessary, and**** _popular_****.** But each one is going NOWHERE until the Senate repeals the Jim Crow filibuster — and stops allowing Mitch McConnell to have the power to block any and every piece of the people's agenda.
[**I'm calling on the Senate to take immediate action because the change voters demanded last year can NOT wait. But I can’t do this alone. Can you chip in $3 or more to fuel our work to end the filibuster once and for all?**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/2019?refcode=em20210509-276&t=22&akid=276%2E420207%2E5dSXHt)
**[Chip in $3](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/2019?refcode=em20210509-276&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=23&akid=276%2E420207%2E5dSXHt)**
It's clear that if we have any hope of delivering the bold and meaningful reforms that our communities need, the filibuster must GO. It's voting rights OR the filibuster. It's a $15 minimum wage OR the filibuster. It's humane immigration reform OR the filibuster. It's LGBTQ+ equality OR the filibuster. It's racial justice OR the filibuster.
**Our call for reform is not new or radical.** Over the years, the Senate has updated its rules in response to the needs of the times. For example, in 1975, the Senate lowered the threshold for ending a filibuster to 60 Senators. In 2013, the Senate eliminated the filibuster entirely for federal executive branch appointees and judicial appointments. Most recently, it eliminated the filibuster for Supreme Court nominees.
Nearly 100 of my colleagues from across the Democratic caucus are urging the Senate to end the filibuster. This week, members of Congress joined outside groups in hosting events around the country where we discussed the countless ways the filibuster is blocking our progress. **And this is just the start of our work to build the grassroots pressure necessary to finally bring change to the Senate — and to America.**
[**Getting this done won't be easy, but if we can keep building a movement big enough to show senators that the only path to progress is eliminating this outdated roadblock — we will win. Chip in $3 or more now to support this vital work.**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/2019?refcode=em20210509-276&t=24&akid=276%2E420207%2E5dSXHt)
In solidarity,
Pramila

**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-footer?refcode=em20210509-276-footer&t=25&refcode2=276_420207_5dSXHt&akid=276%2E420207%2E5dSXHt)**
---

Email us: info@pramilaforcongress.com
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, and small-dollar contributions sustain our movement for transformative change. To contribute by check, please address to Pramila for Congress, PO Box 21912, Seattle, WA 98111.

* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/did-your-info-change/?t=26&akid=276%2E420207%2E5dSXHt)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=27&akid=276%2E420207%2E5dSXHt)
